title: taraxacum officinale var. palustre
intro: Taraxacum officinale var. palustre, commonly known as marsh dandelion, is a variety of the dandelion plant that grows in wetland areas. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of marsh dandelion include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Marsh dandelion can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in capsule or tablet form, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
